_meta.scenario: post-discharge medication reconciliation — Metformin 48h hold after CT contrast

encounter.type: elective imaging — abdomen CT with IV contrast

discharge_summary.text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Aaron Rodriguez Synthea-7 (DOB 1962-03-22)
Encounter: E456 (2026-05-09 to 2026-05-11)
Procedure: CT abdomen/pelvis with IV iodinated contrast on 2026-05-09.

Medication changes at discharge:
  - HOLD Metformin 1000 mg BID for 48 hours following IV contrast administration. Restart 2026-05-12 morning AFTER kidney function recheck. Reduce restart dose if eGFR < 60 (current: 58).
  - STOP Lisinopril 10 mg daily due to ACE-inhibitor-induced cough.
  - START Losartan 50 mg daily (ARB substitute for ACE-I — same indication, no cough cross-reactivity expected).
  - START Atorvastatin 40 mg at bedtime for ASCVD primary prevention given LDL elevated on admission labs.

Follow up with PCP in 2 weeks. Recheck eGFR before restarting Metformin. Patient educated on signs of bleeding (NSAID avoidance) and warned about Losartan starting dose hypotension.
